title: Lateral Movement: Network Logon from Multiple Hosts to Single Target
description: Detects Event 4624 LogonType 3 (network) patterns where multiple source hosts authenticate to a single target within a short window — indicates lateral movement sweep or credential spraying across the domain.
query: 'Log Source' = 'Windows Security Events' and 'Event ID' = '4624' and 'Original Log Content' like '%LogonType:3%' and not 'Original Log Content' like '%TargetUserName:ANONYMOUS LOGON%' and not 'Original Log Content' like '%TargetUserName:-$%' | eval Account = regexextract('Original Log Content', 'TargetUserName:([^;]+)'), SrcIP = regexextract('Original Log Content', 'IpAddress:([^;]+)') | stats distinctcount(SrcIP) as UniqueSources, count as TotalLogons by Account, Entity | where UniqueSources > 3 | sort -UniqueSources
falsepositives: Domain admin accounts authenticating from multiple workstations
